title: Nicolas X. Cloutier
content:
People.ContactEmail ncloutier@mccarthy.ca
Nicolas Cloutier helps clients resolve strategic tax disputes and complex regulatory matters involving government, where business objectives, public authorities and legal frameworks intersect.
Nicolas is a partner in McCarthy Tétrault’s Montréal office. His practice focuses on tax litigation and the resolution of disputes with tax authorities, particularly in matters where financial, reputational or operational stakes require strategic approaches and creative solutions.
Nicolas acts at every stage of tax disputes, including information requests, audits, searches, objections, appeals and collection measures. He represents clients in income tax, sales tax and property tax matters before Revenu Québec, the Canada Revenue Agency, municipal authorities and the courts.
With more than 25 years of experience, Nicolas understands how tax administrations analyze files, exercise their powers and assess risk. This knowledge allows him to intervene early, identify the real issues and propose realistic and effective solutions.
Nicolas also acts in other complex disputes with government, particularly where regulatory frameworks impede the realization of major projects or where business models evolve more quickly than public policy. He helps clients resolve these situations in a practical way, by aligning business imperatives with the requirements of public authorities.
Nicolas designs and negotiates multi-faceted solutions, including complex agreements, governmental or regulatory measures and the targeted use of litigation. He acts on projects involving natural resources and industry, public transit, and major real estate development or revitalization projects.
Nicolas has appeared before all levels of courts in Canada, in both French and English. He is also an active speaker on tax administration, the powers of public authorities, new technologies and administrative law.
Nicolas is a member of the board of directors and vice-president of the Association de planification fiscale et financière. He obtained his Bachelor of Civil Law and Bachelor of Laws from McGill University in 1996 and was called to the Québec Bar in 1999.
